title: secure_zero word-aligned path has redundant alignment check
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_util.h:51-52

## Finding
The word-aligned write loop in `secure_zero()` checks alignment twice:
```c
while (len >= sizeof(unsigned long) &&
       ((uintptr_t)p & (sizeof(unsigned long) - 1)) == 0) {
```

The alignment check in the inner while condition is always true after the initial alignment loop (lines 46-49) completes. This is harmless but redundant.

## What goes wrong
Nothing - the code is correct. The redundant check adds a few extra cycles per iteration on the hot path. For security-critical code paths holding mutexes (as noted in project-LICHEN-gy7h.18), this could be optimized.

## Recommendation
Low priority. If optimizing, remove the redundant alignment check from the while condition:
```c
/* At this point p is word-aligned due to the loop above */
while (len >= sizeof(unsigned long)) {
    *(volatile unsigned long *)p = 0;
    ...
}
```

Or add a comment explaining why the check is there (e.g., defensive coding).
